ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 210023638) FILED BY DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE THE PARTIES STIPULATED JUDGMENT REGARDING PLAINTIFFS' THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND THE COURT'S ORDER THEREON IS DENIED. THE REQUESTED RELIEF IS PREMATURE UNDER THE SOLE AUTHORITY CITED BY THE MOVING PARTY. IN JOHNSTONE THE COURT DISREGARDED THE PARTIES' STIPULATION OF FACTS REGARDING THE LOCATION OF THE ROAD/EASEMENT AFTER IT HAS BEEN CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINED BY SAID COURT THAT THE ROAD WAS IN A DIFFERENT LOCATION. NO SUCH CONCLUSIVE DETERMINATION HAS BEEN MADE BY A TRIER OF FACT IN THIS ACTION AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF THE ENCROACHMENT. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; REPORTED: SHEILA PHAM; CSR# 13293; 415-274-9977; CALENDAR-LA@VERITEXT.COM (D501)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside And Vacate The Parties Stipulated Judgment Regarding Plaintiffs' Third Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief And The Court'S Order Thereon
Off Calendar Oct-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Jennifer Slater 626-577-9500 of Garret & Tully, P.C. appearing via Zoom for defendant.
Zachary Young 926-600-1800 of Patton Sullivan Brodehl, LLP appearing via Zoom for plaintiffs.
Reporter: Sheila Pham; CSR# 13293; 415-274-9977; calendar-la@veritext.com

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details).Defendant contests tentative. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside And Vacate The Parties Stipulated Judgment Regarding Plaintiffs' Third Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief And The Court'S Order Thereon

is DENIED. The requested relief is premature under the sole authority cited by the moving party. In Johnstone the court disregarded the parties' stipulation of facts regarding the location of the road/easement after it has been conclusively determined by said court that the road was in a different location. No such conclusive determination has been made by a trier of fact in this action as to the existence of the encroachment.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Reported: Sheila Pham; CSR# 13293; 415-274-9977; calendar-la@veritext.com (D501)
